revision.text: '''坂口王子'''は熊野古道2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。大阪市中央区神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。
'''坂口王子'''は熊野古道2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。大阪市中央区神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は榎とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。一里塚ともいわれている。ちなみに、神体の榎は鑑定によると、槐だそうである。

'''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は榎とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。一里塚ともいわれている。ちなみに、神体の榎は鑑定によると、槐だそうである。

'''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は榎とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。一里塚ともいわれている。ちなみに、神体の榎は鑑定によると、槐だそうである。
*最寄駅 [[御堂筋線]][[本町駅]]

'''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。ここには、昔朝日神明宮という神社があって、今は[[此花区]]にある。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は榎とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。一里塚ともいわれている。ちなみに、神体の榎は鑑定によると、槐だそうである。
*最寄駅 *南大江公園と榎木大明神 [[御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] *朝日神明宮 [[西日本旅客鉄道]][[ゆめ咲線]][[安治川口駅]]

'''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。ここには、昔朝日神明宮という神社があって、今は[[此花区]]にある。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は榎とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。一里塚ともいわれている。ちなみに、神体の榎は鑑定によると、槐だそうである。

*南大江公園と榎木大明神 [[御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] *朝日神明宮 [[西日本旅客鉄道]][[ゆめ咲線]][[安治川口駅]]

[[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|朝日神明社跡・坂口王子伝承地]] '''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。ここには昔、[[朝日神明社|朝日神明宮]]という神社があって、今は[[此花区]]にある。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は[[エノキ|榎]]とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。[[一里塚]]ともいわれている。神体の榎は鑑定によると、[[エンジュ|槐]]だそうである。 [[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|狸坂大明神]]
== 最寄駅 == *南大江公園と榎木大明神 [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]][[松屋町駅]]または[[谷町六丁目駅]] *朝日神明宮 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜島線|JRゆめ咲線]][[安治川口駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] : [[窪津王子]] - '''坂口王子''' - [[郡戸王子]] {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さかくちおうし}} [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * **Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * **Asahi Shinmeigū:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}

<noinclude> [[ja:坂口王子]][[zh:坂口王子]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]] </noinclude> [[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:坂口王子]][[zh:坂口王子]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * **Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * **Asahi Shinmeigū:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Korudo Ōji|ja|郡戸王子}}

revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}
{{citation needed|Sakaguchi Ouji}} [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:坂口王子]][[zh:坂口王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:akaguchi Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaguchi Ōji]][[Category:11]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}
{{citation needed|Sakaguchi Ouji}} [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
'''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園}} in {{ill|Kanzaki-chō|ja|神崎町}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|ja|此花区}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|enoki|ja|エノキ}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|ja|一里塚}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}
{{citation needed|Sakaguchi Ouji}} [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐}} (Japanese pagoda tree).

== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|ja|Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|ja|松屋町駅}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|ja|谷町六丁目駅}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|ja|桜島線|JRゆめ咲線}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|ja|安治川口駅}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
[[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|朝日神明社跡・坂口王子伝承地]] '''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。ここには昔、[[朝日神明社|朝日神明宮]]という神社があって、今は[[此花区]]にある。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は[[エノキ|榎]]とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。[[一里塚]]ともいわれている。神体の榎は鑑定によると、[[エンジュ|槐]]だそうである。 [[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|狸坂大明神]]
== 最寄駅 == *南大江公園と榎木大明神 [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]][[松屋町駅]]または[[谷町六丁目駅]] *朝日神明宮 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜島線|JRゆめ咲線]][[安治川口駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] : [[窪津王子]] - '''坂口王子''' - [[郡戸王子]] {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さかくちおうし}} [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]]

[[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|朝日神明社跡・坂口王子伝承地]] '''坂口王子'''（'''さかぐちおうじ'''）は、[[熊野古道]]2番目の[[九十九王子]]。現在消滅。[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]神崎町の南大江公園周辺が跡地と思われる。ここには昔、[[朝日神明社|朝日神明宮]]という神社があって、今は[[此花区]]にある。現在、その南大江公園には狸坂大明神の祠がある。そのちかくには、王子跡とされている榎木大明神がある。榎木大明神は[[エノキ|榎]]とされている木を神体とした祠で、南大江公園の南に鎮座している。[[一里塚]]ともいわれている。神体の榎は鑑定によると、[[エンジュ|槐]]だそうである。 [[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|狸坂大明神]]
== 最寄駅 == *南大江公園と榎木大明神 [[Osaka Metro長堀鶴見緑地線]][[松屋町駅]]または[[谷町六丁目駅]] *朝日神明宮 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜島線|JRゆめ咲線]][[安治川口駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] : [[窪津王子]] - '''坂口王子''' - [[郡戸王子]] {{神道 横}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|qq=|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|qq=|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|qq=|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|qq=|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|qq=|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}
