revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kōzaki|Kanzaki-chō]], [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]].
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to [[:en:Konohana-ku, Osaka|Konohana-ku, Osaka]]. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an [[:en:Celtis sinensis|enoki]] (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient [[:en:Ichirizuka|Ichirizuka]] (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' [[:en:Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line]] – [[:en:Matsuyamachi Station|Matsuyamachi Station]] or [[:en:Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|Tanimachi Rokuchome Station]] * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Sakurajima Line|Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)]] – [[:en:Ajikawaguchi Station|Ajikawaguchi Station]] == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|en|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|WD=Q284828|lt=Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|en|Matsuyamachi Station|WD=Q126080|lt=Matsuyamachi Station}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|WD=Q1041967|lt=Tanimachi Rokuchome Station}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line|en|Sakurajima Line|WD=Q1066239|lt=Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|en|Ajikawaguchi Station|WD=Q1042791|lt=Ajikawaguchi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11425069]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|en|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|WD=Q284828|lt=Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|en|Matsuyamachi Station|WD=Q126080|lt=Matsuyamachi Station}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|WD=Q1041967|lt=Tanimachi Rokuchome Station}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line|en|Sakurajima Line|WD=Q1066239|lt=Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|en|Ajikawaguchi Station|WD=Q1042791|lt=Ajikawaguchi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|en|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|WD=Q284828|lt=Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|en|Matsuyamachi Station|WD=Q126080|lt=Matsuyamachi Station}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|WD=Q1041967|lt=Tanimachi Rokuchome Station}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line|en|Sakurajima Line|WD=Q1066239|lt=Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|en|Ajikawaguchi Station|WD=Q1042791|lt=Ajikawaguchi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}} – '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' – {{ill|Kōto Ōji|ja|郡戸王子|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōto Ōji|1=Kōto Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643221}} {{Shinto Sidebar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=enju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō|1=Kōzaki}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka|1=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki|1=Celtis sinensis}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka|1=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=Q11541992|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
== Nearest Stations == * '''Minami-Oe Park and Enoki Daimyōjin:''' {{ill|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|en|Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|WD=Q284828|lt=Osaka Metro Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line|1=Nagahori Tsurumi-ryokuchi Line}} – {{ill|Matsuyamachi Station|en|Matsuyamachi Station|WD=Q126080|lt=Matsuyamachi Station|1=Matsuyamachi Station}} or {{ill|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|en|Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station|WD=Q1041967|lt=Tanimachi Rokuchome Station|1=Tanimachi Rokuchōme Station}} * '''Asahi Shinmeigū:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Sakurajima Line|en|Sakurajima Line|WD=Q1066239|lt=Sakurajima Line (JR Yumesaki Line)|1=Sakurajima Line}} – {{ill|Ajikawaguchi Station|en|Ajikawaguchi Station|WD=Q1042791|lt=Ajikawaguchi Station|1=Ajikawaguchi Station}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:坂口王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== [[File:Site of Asahi-sinmeisha, Sakaguchi-Oji.JPG|thumb|Site of Asahi Shinmeisha, Sakaguchi Ōji]] '''Sakaguchi Ōji''' ('''さかぐちおうじ''') was the second {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrine along the {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route. The shrine no longer exists. Its presumed site is around {{ill|Minami-Oe Park|ja|南大江公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Oe Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kanzaki-chō|1=Kōzaki}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}.
There used to be a shrine called {{ill|Asahi Shinmeigū|ja|朝日神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Shinmeigū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in this area, which has since been relocated to {{ill|Konohana-ku, Osaka|en|Konohana-ku, Osaka|WD=Q960258|lt=Konohana-ku, Osaka|1=Konohana-ku, Osaka}}. Currently, a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja|狸坂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanukizaka Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stands within Minami-Oe Park. Nearby is the {{ill|Enoki Daimyōjin|ja|榎木大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoki Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered a remnant of the Ōji site. Enoki Daimyōjin is a small shrine enshrining a sacred tree, believed to be an {{ill|Celtis sinensis|en|Celtis sinensis|WD=Q706339|lt=enoki|1=Celtis sinensis}} (Chinese hackberry), and is located just south of Minami-Oe Park. Some also consider it an ancient {{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|WD=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka|1=Ichirizuka}} (milestone mound).
However, botanical analysis has revealed that the sacred tree is actually an {{ill|enju|ja|槐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enju|1=Q11541992|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541992}} (Japanese pagoda tree).
[[File:TanukizakaDaimyojin.JPG|thumb|Tanukizaka Daimyōjin]]
